questions.prompt:
The amount of hearing loss a person experiences is based on hearing sensitivity. Hearing sensitivity is determined by measuring the absolute threshold for hearing. The graph shows the hearing thresholds for individuals in different age groups, measured in decibels.1

Hearing Thresholds at Different Frequencies by Age Group

  • The following hearing ranges are indicated:
    • 0 to 20 decibels: Normal Hearing Range
    • 20 to 40 decibels: Mild Hearing Loss Range
    • 40 to 70 decibels: Moderate Hearing Loss Range
    • 70 to 90 decibels: Severe Hearing Loss Range
    • 90 to 100 decibels: Profound Hearing Loss Range
  • The following 4 lines representing ages are shown:
    • 20 years
    • 40 years
    • 60 years
    • 90 years
  • All values are approximate.
  • The 20 years line:
    • Begins at frequency 125, 0 decibels
    • Remains level to frequency 1,000, 0 decibels
    • Falls gradually to frequency 8,000, 10 decibels
  • The 40 years line:
    • Begins at frequency 125, 7 decibels
    • Falls gradually to frequency 1,000, 8 decibels
    • Falls gradually to frequency 8,000, 31 decibels
  • The 60 years line:
    • Begins at frequency 125, 15 decibels
    • Remains level to frequency 1,000, 15 decibels
    • Falls gradually to frequency 2,000, 25 decibels
    • Falls sharply to frequency 8,000, 72 decibels
  • The 90 years line:
    • Begins at frequency 125, 22 decibels
    • Falls gradually to frequency 1,000, 23 decibels
    • Falls gradually to frequency 2,000, 36 decibels
    • Falls sharply to frequency 8,000, 100 decibels
  • A note indicates the figure is not drawn to scale.
1: A sound’s amplitude is measured on the decibel scale. The more intense, or louder the sound is, the higher the decibel level. The human threshold for hearing is 0 decibels.↩
Which of the following statements is best supported by the graph?

options.content: People in their 20s have more difficulty hearing higher frequency sounds than people in their 60s.
People who are 90 years old show no difficulty hearing frequencies at the 2,000 level when played at the 40 decibel level.
As people get older, their ability to hear higher frequency sounds decreases.
In general, a sixty-year-old person cannot hear higher decibel levels at higher frequencies.

questions.prompt:
Researchers were exploring depth perception in infants. In one study, researchers built a plexiglass box that appeared to have a ledge with a three-foot drop. There was no actual drop that could injure the infants.

The infants were randomly assigned to one of two groups. In the first group, each infant was encouraged to crawl over the ledge toward their mother, who was present on the other side of the box. In the second group, each infant could crawl around on the plexiglass box, but their mother was absent.

Researchers measured how many infants crawled over the ledge to get to the other side of the box. The results are presented in the graph.

Percentage of Infants Crawling Over the Ledge by Condition

  • The Percentage Crawling over the Ledge data for the 2 bars are as follows:
    • With Mother Present: 57%
    • Without Mother Present: 70%

Which of the following statements best describes why this study is experimental?

options.content: The infants who participated in the study were a representative sample.
The infants who participated in the study were randomly assigned to one of two conditions.
Observation took place in a natural setting where the researchers did not interfere with the behavior of the infants who participated in the study.
The two variables were empirically shown to be positively correlated.

    • Without Mother Present: 70%

Which of the following is the dependent variable in this study?

options.content: The percentage of infants who crawled over the perceived ledge
The total number of infants who participated
Whether the mother was present while each infant crawled on the plexiglass box
The plexiglass box that was used

questions.prompt:
Researchers were exploring depth perception in infants. In one study, researchers built a plexiglass box that appeared to have a ledge with a three-foot drop. There was no actual drop that could injure the infants.

The infants were randomly assigned to one of two groups. In the first group, each infant was encouraged to crawl over the ledge toward their mother, who was present on the other side of the box. In the second group, each infant could crawl around on the plexiglass box, but their mother was absent.

Researchers measured how many infants crawled over the ledge to get to the other side of the box. The results are presented in the graph.

Percentage of Infants Crawling Over the Ledge by Condition

  • All values are approximate.
  • The Percentage Crawling over the Ledge data for the 2 bars are as follows:
    • With Mother Present: 57%
    • Without Mother Present: 70%

This study is most directly tied to which of the following research areas?

questions.prompt:
Some psychoactive drugs increase neurotransmitter availability along the reward center pathway by trapping neurotransmitters in the synapse, the gap between neurons. This process creates a sense of well-being.

Which of the following processes is most likely occurring?

options.content: The drug acts as a reuptake inhibitor.
The drug acts as an antagonist.
The drug no longer works effectively because the body has developed a tolerance to it.
The drug only works because of the placebo effect.

questions.prompt:
Dr. Hains conducted a memory experiment. Participants were randomly assigned to one of two groups. Group 1 was given a list of words that were not related to one another. Group 2 was given a list of words that were related to one another. In both lists, each word was listed once. Both groups were given one minute to memorize the list. After a five-minute pause, participants were asked to write down as many words as they could recall.

What was the independent variable in this experiment?

options.content: The type of word list
The number of words participants could remember
The random assignment of participants to groups
The five-minute break between encoding the words and recalling the words
